name: X-Force: Mail Server Sending Mail to Server Categorized as SPAM
notes:
This rule triggers when a local mail server communicates with an IP that is considered to be sending SPAM. Usually there is no reason for internal hosts to be communicating with known SPAM hosts.

Note: The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Remote
when a flow or an event matches all of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers, BB:PortDefinition: Mail Ports
when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Spam with confidence value greater than 75
TD_list: APPLY X-Force: Mail Server Sending Mail to Server Categorized as SPAM on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Remote
AND when a flow or an event matches all of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers, BB:PortDefinition: Mail Ports
AND when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Spam with confidence value greater than 75
RED_list: Event name: Mail Server Sending Mail to Servers Categorized as SPAM

Event description: This rule will notify when an internal mail server, communicates with an IP that is considered to be sending SPAM. Usually there is no reason for internal hosts to be communicating with known SPAM hosts.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
